dataset: Claude Shannon's historical Master's thesis (public historical record)

pi_items.metadata_value: A Symbolic Analysis of Relay and Switching Circuits
pi_items.verified_value: A Symbolic Analysis of Relay and Switching Circuits
pi_items.source: Multiple authoritative sources (MIT DSpace, Wikipedia, Britannica) confirm Shannon's thesis title
Verified full name is Claude Elwood Shannon; last name is Shannon (confirmed via multiple biographic sources)

MIT records show thesis submitted August 10, 1937; published in Transactions of the American Institute of Electrical Engineers vol. 57 in 1938; Shannon received his Master's degree in 1940. For bibliographic purposes, 1937 or 1938 are the standard citations.
Confirmed via MIT DSpace repository (dspace.mit.edu), MIT News archives, and multiple academic sources
Multiple sources confirm this was Shannon's Master's thesis at MIT. He later earned a PhD in Mathematics from MIT in 1940.

privileged_info_summary: Claude Shannon submitted his Master's thesis 'A Symbolic Analysis of Relay and Switching Circuits' to Massachusetts Institute of Technology in 1937, published in 1938, and received his degree in 1940. The title, author (Claude Elwood Shannon), university, and thesis type are all historically accurate. The year in the metadata (1940) refers to when Shannon received his degree, but the thesis itself was written in 1937 and published in 1938—this discrepancy should be noted for bibliographic accuracy.
